figures.caption: Table 4: Paradigm 1 results: text readout with frozen BERT (ε = 0.05). ACC = clustering accuracy (Hungarian); NMI = normalised mutual information; ARI = adjusted Rand index; best epoch reported.
Table 1: Structural comparison of prototype based attention mechanisms.

Figure 4: Exp 2: DDCL-Attention soft VQ on CIFAR-10 (K = 64, 50 epochs). Left: training dynamics (loss decomposition, V ≥ 0, codebook utilisation). Right: codebook utilisation over epochs for DDCL-Attention vs. hard VQ-VAE; DDCL-Attention achieves 100% from epoch 1 while VQ-VAE requires 26 epochs.
Table 2: Five application paradigms for DDCL-Attention. ✓ = validated in this paper.

Figure 2: Exp 1: training dynamics for SST-2 (top), IMDB (middle), and 20 Newsgroups (bottom). Each row shows the loss decomposition Lq = LOLS + V (top left), variance term V ≥ 0 (top centre), prototype separation S(P) (top right), assignment entropy H(Q) (bottom left), clustering quality ACC/NMI/ARI (bottom centre), and phase portrait (V/N,S(P)) (bottom right).
